Scorsese directed the pilot for *[Vinyl](/wiki/Vinyl_(TV_series) "Vinyl (TV series)")* written by Terence Winter and [George Mastras](/wiki/George_Mastras "George Mastras"), with [Mick Jagger](/wiki/Mick_Jagger "Mick Jagger") producing and Mastras as [showrunner](/wiki/Showrunner "Showrunner"). The series stars [Bobby Cannavale](/wiki/Bobby_Cannavale "Bobby Cannavale") as Richie Finestra, founder and president of a top-tier record label, set in 1970s New York City's drug-and sex-fueled music business as punk and disco were breaking out, all told through the eyes of Finestra trying to resurrect his label and find the next new sound. Filming began on July 25, 2014.[[139]](#cite_note-139) Co-stars include [Ray Romano](/wiki/Ray_Romano "Ray Romano"), [Olivia Wilde](/wiki/Olivia_Wilde "Olivia Wilde"), [Juno Temple](/wiki/Juno_Temple "Juno Temple"), [Andrew Dice Clay](/wiki/Andrew_Dice_Clay "Andrew Dice Clay"), [Ato Essandoh](/wiki/Ato_Essandoh "Ato Essandoh"), [Max Casella](/wiki/Max_Casella "Max Casella"), and James Jagger. On December 2, 2014, *Vinyl* was picked up by HBO.[[140]](#cite_note-140) The series lasted one season. Scorsese has acted as executive producer of several indie films, like the 2014 *[The Third Side of the River](/wiki/The_Third_Side_of_the_River "The Third Side of the River")* (directed by his protege Celina Murga), another 2014 film *[Revenge of the Green Dragons](/wiki/Revenge_of_the_Green_Dragons "Revenge of the Green Dragons")* (co-directed by Andrew Lau, whose film *Infernal Affairs* inspired *The Departed*),[[141]](#cite_note-141) as well as *[Bleed for This](/wiki/Bleed_for_This "Bleed for This")* and *[Free Fire](/wiki/Free_Fire_(film) "Free Fire (film)")*.[[142]](#cite_note-142)

Scorsese directed *[The Audition](/wiki/The_Audition_(2015_film) "The Audition (2015 film)")*, a short film that also served as a promotional piece for casinos [Studio City](/wiki/Studio_City_(Macau) "Studio City (Macau)") in [Macau](/wiki/Macau "Macau") and [City of Dreams](/wiki/City_of_Dreams_Manila "City of Dreams Manila") in [Manila, Philippines](/wiki/Manila,_Philippines "Manila, Philippines"). The short brought together Scorsese's long-time muses Leonardo DiCaprio and Robert De Niro for the first time under his direction. The short film featured the two actors, playing fictionalized versions of themselves, competing for a role in Scorsese's next film. It was Scorsese's first collaboration with De Niro in two decades.[[143]](#cite_note-143) The film premiered in October 2015 in conjunction with the grand opening of Studio City.[[144]](#cite_note-reuters-144)

### 2016–present

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2016–present")]

Scorsese had long anticipated filming an adaptation of [Shūsaku Endō](/wiki/Sh%C5%ABsaku_End%C5%8D "Shūsaku Endō")'s novel *[Silence](/wiki/Silence_(End%C5%8D_novel) "Silence (Endō novel)")*, a drama about the lives of two Portuguese [Jesuit](/wiki/Jesuit "Jesuit") priests in Japan during the 17th century. He had originally planned *Silence* as his next project following *Shutter Island.*[[145]](#cite_note-145) On April 19, 2013, financing was secured for *[Silence](/wiki/Silence_(2016_film) "Silence (2016 film)")* by [Emmett/Furla Films](/wiki/Emmett/Furla_Films "Emmett/Furla Films"),[[146]](#cite_note-146) and filming began in January 2015. By November 2016, the film had completed post-production. It was written by Jay Cocks and Scorsese, based on the novel, and stars [Andrew Garfield](/wiki/Andrew_Garfield "Andrew Garfield"), [Liam Neeson](/wiki/Liam_Neeson "Liam Neeson"), and [Adam Driver](/wiki/Adam_Driver "Adam Driver").[[147]](#cite_note-147) The film was released on December 23, 2016, to positive reviews from critics.[[148]](#cite_note-148)[[149]](#cite_note-149) Scorsese was recognized as an Italian citizen by *[jus sanguinis](/wiki/Jus_sanguinis "Jus sanguinis")* in 2018.[[150]](#cite_note-cittadino-italiano-150)

On January 10, 2019, *Variety*'s Chris Willman reported that Scorsese's long-anticipated documentary of Bob Dylan's 1975 tour, the [Rolling Thunder Revue](/wiki/Rolling_Thunder_Revue "Rolling Thunder Revue"), would be released by [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix"): "*[Rolling Thunder Revue: A Bob Dylan Story by Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Rolling_Thunder_Revue:_A_Bob_Dylan_Story_by_Martin_Scorsese "Rolling Thunder Revue: A Bob Dylan Story by Martin Scorsese")* captures the troubled spirit of America in 1975 and the joyous music that Dylan performed during the fall of that year. Part documentary, part concert film, part fever dream, *Rolling Thunder* is a one of a kind experience, from master filmmaker Martin Scorsese."[[151]](#cite_note-151) On April 25, 2019, it was announced that the documentary would be released on Netflix on June 12, 2019, with a concurrent theatrical engagement in twenty American, European, and Australian cities the night before, and an extended theatrical schedule in Los Angeles and New York so that the film will qualify for award consideration.[[152]](#cite_note-152)[[153]](#cite_note-153) After years of development, principal photography on Scorsese's crime film *[The Irishman](/wiki/The_Irishman "The Irishman")*, based on the book *[I Heard You Paint Houses](/wiki/I_Heard_You_Paint_Houses "I Heard You Paint Houses")* by [Charles Brandt](/wiki/Charles_Brandt "Charles Brandt"), began in August 2017, starring Robert De Niro, Joe Pesci, and [Al Pacino](/wiki/Al_Pacino "Al Pacino").[[154]](#cite_note-154) The film had its world premiere at the [57th New York Film Festival](/wiki/57th_New_York_Film_Festival "57th New York Film Festival") on September 27, 2019.[[155]](#cite_note-155) It received a limited theatrical release on November 1, 2019, followed by digital streaming on November 27, 2019, on Netflix.[[156]](#cite_note-156) In January 2020, *The Irishman* received ten [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award "Academy Award") nominations, including for [Best Picture](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Picture "Academy Award for Best Picture"), [Best Director](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Director "Academy Award for Best Director"), [Best Adapted Screenplay](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Adapted_Screenplay "Academy Award for Best Adapted Screenplay"), and [Best Supporting Actor](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actor "Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor") for Pacino and Pesci.[[157]](#cite_note-157)

On December 29, 2020, the trailer for Scorsese's [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") documentary series *[Pretend It's a City](/wiki/Pretend_It%27s_a_City "Pretend It's a City")* was released. The series features [Fran Lebowitz](/wiki/Fran_Lebowitz "Fran Lebowitz") and Scorsese as they delve into her personal beliefs and thoughts on New York City.[[158]](#cite_note-158) The project was released January 8, 2021, on Netflix.[[159]](#cite_note-159) This is Scorsese's second documentary featuring Lebowitz, the first being *[Public Speaking](/wiki/Public_Speaking_(film) "Public Speaking (film)")* (2010), which was released on [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO").[[160]](#cite_note-160) In October 2022, Scorsese and David Tedeschi premiered their collaborative film *Personality Crisis: One Night Only*, at the New York Film Festival. The film is a documentary about [David Johansen](/wiki/David_Johansen "David Johansen"), featuring both contemporary concert footage shot for the project as well as archival footage.[[161]](#cite_note-161)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f6/ScorseseScreentalk071023_%286_of_27%29_%2853241781347%29.jpg/250px-ScorseseScreentalk071023_%286_of_27%29_%2853241781347%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:ScorseseScreentalk071023_(6_of_27)_(53241781347).jpg)

Scorsese with [Edgar Wright](/wiki/Edgar_Wright "Edgar Wright") at the [London Film Festival](/wiki/London_Film_Festival "London Film Festival") in 2023

In July 2019, Scorsese started scouting locations in preparation for the 2020 filming of his next film *[Killers of the Flower Moon](/wiki/Killers_of_the_Flower_Moon "Killers of the Flower Moon")*, a film adaptation of the [book of the same name](/wiki/Killers_of_the_Flower_Moon_(book) "Killers of the Flower Moon (book)") by [David Grann](/wiki/David_Grann "David Grann"). Scorsese would [team up with Leonardo DiCaprio for the sixth time](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese_and_Leonardo_DiCaprio "Martin Scorsese and Leonardo DiCaprio") and [Robert De Niro for the tenth time](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese_and_Robert_De_Niro "Martin Scorsese and Robert De Niro").[[162]](#cite_note-162) In December 2019, Scorsese's frequent cinematographer [Rodrigo Prieto](/wiki/Rodrigo_Prieto "Rodrigo Prieto") confirmed that *Flower Moon* was gearing up to start principal photography in March 2020, which was postponed due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[163]](#cite_note-163) In November 2020 Scorsese was hounored with Stockholm Lifetime Achievement Award. In April 2020, it was announced that filming for *Killers of the Flower Moon* had been postponed indefinitely in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, that the potential cost of the film had ballooned to $200 million, and that Scorsese was in talks with [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") or [Apple](/wiki/Apple_Inc. "Apple Inc.") to produce and distribute, with [Paramount Pictures](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures") involved as a partner.[[164]](#cite_note-164)[[165]](#cite_note-165) On May 27, 2020, Apple bought the production and distribution rights to the film, which was released theatrically by Paramount and streamed on [Apple TV](/wiki/Apple_TV_(streaming_service) "Apple TV (streaming service)").[[166]](#cite_note-166) Principal photography commenced in April 2021.[[167]](#cite_note-167) It premiered at the [76th Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/76th_Cannes_Film_Festival "76th Cannes Film Festival") on May 20, 2023, receiving a nine-minute standing ovation and was released theatrically on October 20, 2023.[[168]](#cite_note-168) On May 16, 2023, while promoting *Killers of the Flower Moon*, Scorsese spoke about his eagerness to continue working, stating: "I'm old. I read stuff. I see things. I want to tell stories, and there's no more time."[[169]](#cite_note-169) It was nominated for ten Oscars, and seven Golden Globes, winning one for Best Actress in a Drama for [Lily Gladstone](/wiki/Lily_Gladstone "Lily Gladstone"). That same year, he directed a commercial for [Bleu de Chanel](/wiki/Bleu_de_Chanel "Bleu de Chanel") starring [Timothée Chalamet](/wiki/Timoth%C3%A9e_Chalamet "Timothée Chalamet").[[170]](#cite_note-170)

In 2024, Scorsese narrated the documentary film *[Made in England: The Films of Powell and Pressburger](/wiki/Made_in_England:_The_Films_of_Powell_and_Pressburger "Made in England: The Films of Powell and Pressburger")* directed by David Hinton. Guy Lodge of *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* wrote: "Scorsese may not have directed this engaging tour through a vital filmography, but he narrates with palpable, personal devotion to his subject."[[171]](#cite_note-171) The film premiered at the [Berlin International Film Festival](/wiki/Berlin_International_Film_Festival "Berlin International Film Festival") where it was picked up for distribution by [MUBI](/wiki/MUBI "MUBI").[[172]](#cite_note-172)

#### Upcoming projects

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Upcoming projects")]

In November 2021, Scorsese was set to direct a biopic feature about rock band the [Grateful Dead](/wiki/Grateful_Dead "Grateful Dead") for [Apple Studios](/wiki/Apple_TV_(streaming_service) "Apple TV (streaming service)") featuring [Jonah Hill](/wiki/Jonah_Hill "Jonah Hill").[[173]](#cite_note-173) In July 2022, it was announced Scorsese would direct an adaptation of David Grann's non-fiction novel *[The Wager](/wiki/The_Wager:_A_Tale_of_Shipwreck,_Mutiny_and_Murder "The Wager: A Tale of Shipwreck, Mutiny and Murder")* for [Apple Studios](/wiki/Apple_Studios "Apple Studios"), reteaming once again with DiCaprio.[[174]](#cite_note-174) In May 2023, after meeting with [Pope Francis](/wiki/Pope_Francis "Pope Francis"), Scorsese said he was considering writing and directing a new film about Jesus.[[175]](#cite_note-175) More details were revealed in January 2024, revealing that it could be Scorsese's next film and that he had co-written it with [Kent Jones](/wiki/Kent_Jones_(critic) "Kent Jones (critic)"), based on the [Shūsaku Endō](/wiki/Sh%C5%ABsaku_End%C5%8D "Shūsaku Endō") novel *A Life of Jesus*.[[176]](#cite_note-176) In 2023, during an interview with *[GQ](/wiki/GQ "GQ")*, it was revealed that Scorsese was working on an adaptation of [Marilynne Robinson](/wiki/Marilynne_Robinson "Marilynne Robinson")'s *[Home](/wiki/Home_(Robinson_novel) "Home (Robinson novel)")* with [Todd Field](/wiki/Todd_Field "Todd Field") and Jones.[[177]](#cite_note-177) In March 2025, it was reported that he would adapt *Home* for Apple TV with DiCaprio starring.[[178]](#cite_note-178) It was reported in February 2025 that Scorsese would be directing a mob film set in [Hawaii](/wiki/Hawaii "Hawaii"), starring and being co-produced by [Dwayne Johnson](/wiki/Dwayne_Johnson "Dwayne Johnson"), Leonardo DiCaprio and [Emily Blunt](/wiki/Emily_Blunt "Emily Blunt"). Johnson and Blunt pitched the plot to Scorsese and DiCaprio, which was described as being "a cross between *[Goodfellas](/wiki/Goodfellas "Goodfellas")* and *[The Departed](/wiki/The_Departed "The Departed")*", and [Nick Bilton](/wiki/Nick_Bilton "Nick Bilton") was hired as a screenwriter.[[179]](#cite_note-179) On September 18, 2025, it was announced that Scorsese would direct *[What Happens at Night](/wiki/What_Happens_at_Night "What Happens at Night")*, an adaptation of the [Peter Cameron](/wiki/Peter_Cameron_(novelist) "Peter Cameron (novelist)") novel, as his next film, with DiCaprio set to star alongside [Jennifer Lawrence](/wiki/Jennifer_Lawrence "Jennifer Lawrence") which started production in February 2026.[[180]](#cite_note-180) Scorsese was also announced to have a cameo in the next *Star Wars* movie, *[The Mandalorian and Grogu](/wiki/The_Mandalorian_and_Grogu "The Mandalorian and Grogu")*, which is scheduled to be released on May 22, 2026, as the voice of an Ardennian shopkeep.[[181]](#cite_note-181)

## Filmmaking style and technique

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Filmmaking style and technique")]

Several recurring filmmaking techniques are identifiable in many of Scorsese's films. He has established a filmmaking history which involves repeat collaborations with actors, screenwriters, film editors, and cinematographers, sometimes extending over several decades, such as that with recurring cinematographers [Michael Ballhaus](/wiki/Michael_Ballhaus "Michael Ballhaus"), [Robert Richardson](/wiki/Robert_Richardson_(cinematographer) "Robert Richardson (cinematographer)"), and [Rodrigo Prieto](/wiki/Rodrigo_Prieto "Rodrigo Prieto").

### Slow motion and freeze frame

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Slow motion and freeze frame")]

Scorsese is known for his frequent use of [slow motion](/wiki/Slow_motion "Slow motion"), for example, in *Who's That Knocking at My Door* (1967) and *Mean Streets* (1973).[[182]](#cite_note-182) He is also known for using [freeze frames](/wiki/Freeze_frame_shot "Freeze frame shot"), such as: in the opening credits of *The King of Comedy* (1983), throughout *Goodfellas* (1990), *Casino* (1995), *The Departed* (2006), and in *The Irishman* (2019). His blonde leading ladies are usually seen through the eyes of the protagonist as angelic and ethereal; they wear white in their first scene and are photographed in slow motion—[Cybill Shepherd](/wiki/Cybill_Shepherd "Cybill Shepherd") in *Taxi Driver*; [Cathy Moriarty](/wiki/Cathy_Moriarty "Cathy Moriarty")'s white bikini in *Raging Bull*; Sharon Stone's white minidress in *Casino*.[[183]](#cite_note-183) This may be a nod to director Alfred Hitchcock.[[184]](#cite_note-184)

Scorsese often uses long tracking shots,[[185]](#cite_note-bostonglobe-185) as seen in *Taxi Driver*, *Goodfellas*, *Casino*, *Gangs of New York*, and *Hugo*. [MOS](/wiki/MOS_(filmmaking) "MOS (filmmaking)") sequences set to popular music or voice-over are regularly seen in his films, often involving aggressive camera movement or rapid editing.[[186]](#cite_note-186) Scorsese sometimes highlights characters in a scene with an iris, an homage to 1920s [silent film](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film") cinema (as scenes at the time sometimes used this transition). This effect can be seen in *Casino* (it is used on Sharon Stone and Joe Pesci), *Life Lessons*, *The Departed* (on Matt Damon), and *Hugo*. Some of his films include references/allusions to Westerns, particularly *[Rio Bravo](/wiki/Rio_Bravo_(film) "Rio Bravo (film)")*, *[The Great Train Robbery](/wiki/The_Great_Train_Robbery_(1903_film) "The Great Train Robbery (1903 film)")*, *[Shane](/wiki/Shane_(film) "Shane (film)")*, *[The Searchers](/wiki/The_Searchers "The Searchers")*, and *[The Oklahoma Kid](/wiki/The_Oklahoma_Kid "The Oklahoma Kid")*. Slow motion flashbulbs and accented camera/flash/shutter sounds are often used, as is the song "[Gimme Shelter](/wiki/Gimme_Shelter "Gimme Shelter")" by The Rolling Stones heard in several of Scorsese's films: *Goodfellas*, *Casino*, and *The Departed*.

### Cameo appearances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Cameo appearances")]

Scorsese usually has a quick cameo in his films (*Who's That Knocking at My Door*, *Boxcar Bertha*, *Mean Streets*, *Alice Doesn't Live Here Anymore*, *Taxi Driver*, *The King of Comedy*, *After Hours*, *The Last Temptation of Christ* (albeit hidden under a hood), *The Age of Innocence*, *Gangs of New York*, *Hugo*, *Killers of the Flower Moon*), he is also known to contribute his voice to a film without appearing on screen (e.g. as in *The Aviator* and *The Wolf of Wall Street*). In *The Age of Innocence*, for example, he appears in the non-speaking role of a [large format](/wiki/Large_format "Large format") portrait photographer in one of the passing scenes of the film. He provides the opening voice-over narration in *Mean Streets* and *The Color of Money*; plays the off-screen dressing room attendant in the final scene of *Raging Bull*, and provides the voice of the unseen ambulance dispatcher in *Bringing Out the Dead*.[[187]](#cite_note-187) He also appears as the director of fictional newly formed Vatican Television in the Italian comedy *[In the Pope's Eye](/wiki/In_the_Pope%27s_Eye "In the Pope's Eye")* and played himself in the first episode of the TV series [*The Studio*](/wiki/The_Studio_(TV_series) "The Studio (TV series)").[[188]](#cite_note-188)

### Religious guilt

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Religious guilt")]

[Guilt](/wiki/Guilt_(emotion) "Guilt (emotion)") is a prominent theme in many of his films, as is the role of [Catholicism](/wiki/Catholicism "Catholicism") in creating and dealing with guilt (*Who's That Knocking at My Door*, *Mean Streets*, *Raging Bull*, *Bringing Out the Dead*, *The Departed*, *Shutter Island*, and *The Irishman*). In a similar manner, Scorsese considered *Silence* a "passion project": it had been in development since 1990, two years after the release of his film *The Last Temptation of Christ*, which also contained strongly religious themes.[[189]](#cite_note-189) When asked why he retained interest in a project dealing with strong theological themes for over 26 years, Scorsese said,

> As you get older, ideas go and come. Questions, answers, loss of the answer again and more questions, and this is what really interests me. Yes, the cinema and the people in my life and my family are most important, but ultimately as you get older, there's got to be more ... *Silence* is just something that I'm drawn to in that way. It's been an obsession, it has to be done ... it's a strong, wonderful true story, a thriller in a way, but it deals with those questions.[[190]](#cite_note-Emmett_Furla-190)

### Political corruption

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Political corruption")]

More recently, his films have featured corrupt authority figures, such as policemen in *The Departed*[[191]](#cite_note-191) and politicians in *Gangs of New York*[[192]](#cite_note-192) and *The Aviator*.[[193]](#cite_note-193) He is also known for his liberal usage of profanity, [dark humor](/wiki/Dark_humor "Dark humor"), and violence.[[194]](#cite_note-194)

Scorsese's interest in political corruption as depicted in his films was expanded further in his 2019 film *The Irishman*. [Richard Brody](/wiki/Richard_Brody "Richard Brody") writing for *[The New Yorker](/wiki/The_New_Yorker "The New Yorker")* found the main interpretation of the film to be a dark allegory of a realist reading of American politics and American society stating:

> The real-life Hoffa... (was) a crucial player in both gangland politics and the actual practical politics of the day, and the movie's key through line is the inseparability of those two realms. *The Irishman* is a sociopolitical horror story that views much of modern American history as a continuous crime in motion, in which every level of society—from domestic life through local business through big business through national and international politics—is poisoned by graft and bribery, shady deals and dirty money, threats of violence and its gruesome enactment, and the hard-baked impunity that keeps the entire system running.[[195]](#cite_note-195)

### Frequent collaborators

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Frequent collaborators")]

Further information: [Martin Scorsese and Robert De Niro](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese_and_Robert_De_Niro "Martin Scorsese and Robert De Niro") and [Martin Scorsese and Leonardo DiCaprio](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese_and_Leonardo_DiCaprio "Martin Scorsese and Leonardo DiCaprio")

See also: [List of film director and actor collaborations](/wiki/List_of_film_director_and_actor_collaborations "List of film director and actor collaborations")

Scorsese often casts the same actors in his projects, particularly Robert De Niro, who has collaborated with Scorsese on ten feature films and one short film. Included are the three films (*Taxi Driver*, *Raging Bull*, and *Goodfellas*) that made AFI's 100 Years ... 100 Movies list. Scorsese has often said he thinks De Niro's best work under his direction was Rupert Pupkin in *The King of Comedy*. After the turn of the century, Scorsese found a new muse with younger actor Leonardo DiCaprio, collaborating on six feature films and one short to date.[[196]](#cite_note-196) Several critics have compared Scorsese's new partnership with DiCaprio with his previous one with De Niro.[[197]](#cite_note-197)[[198]](#cite_note-198) Frequent collaborators also include: [Victor Argo](/wiki/Victor_Argo "Victor Argo") (6), Harvey Keitel (6), [Harry Northup](/wiki/Harry_Northup "Harry Northup") (6), [J. C. MacKenzie](/wiki/J._C._MacKenzie "J. C. MacKenzie") (5), Murray Moston (5), [Illeana Douglas](/wiki/Illeana_Douglas "Illeana Douglas") (4), Joe Pesci (4), [Frank Vincent](/wiki/Frank_Vincent "Frank Vincent") (3), [Barry Primus](/wiki/Barry_Primus "Barry Primus") (3), and [Verna Bloom](/wiki/Verna_Bloom "Verna Bloom") (3). Others who have appeared in multiple Scorsese projects include Daniel Day-Lewis, who had become very reclusive to the Hollywood scene, [Alec Baldwin](/wiki/Alec_Baldwin "Alec Baldwin"), Willem Dafoe, Ben Kingsley, Jude Law, [Dick Miller](/wiki/Dick_Miller "Dick Miller"), Liam Neeson, Emily Mortimer, [Jesse Plemons](/wiki/Jesse_Plemons "Jesse Plemons"), [John C. Reilly](/wiki/John_C._Reilly "John C. Reilly"), [David Carradine](/wiki/David_Carradine "David Carradine"), Barbara Hershey, [Kevin Corrigan](/wiki/Kevin_Corrigan "Kevin Corrigan"), [Jake Hoffman](/wiki/Jake_Hoffman_(actor) "Jake Hoffman (actor)"), [Frank Sivero](/wiki/Frank_Sivero "Frank Sivero"), [Ray Winstone](/wiki/Ray_Winstone "Ray Winstone") and [Nick Nolte](/wiki/Nick_Nolte "Nick Nolte"). Before their deaths, Scorsese's parents, Charles Scorsese and [Catherine Scorsese](/wiki/Catherine_Scorsese "Catherine Scorsese"), appeared in bit parts, walk-ons or supporting roles, such as in *Goodfellas*.[[199]](#cite_note-Casillo2006-199)

For his crew, Scorsese frequently worked with editors [Marcia Lucas](/wiki/Marcia_Lucas "Marcia Lucas")[[200]](#cite_note-200) and Thelma Schoonmaker,[[201]](#cite_note-201) cinematographers [Michael Ballhaus](/wiki/Michael_Ballhaus "Michael Ballhaus"),[[202]](#cite_note-202) [Robert Richardson](/wiki/Robert_Richardson_(cinematographer) "Robert Richardson (cinematographer)"), Michael Chapman and [Rodrigo Prieto](/wiki/Rodrigo_Prieto "Rodrigo Prieto"), screenwriters Paul Schrader, Mardik Martin, Jay Cocks, Terrence Winter, [John Logan](/wiki/John_Logan_(writer) "John Logan (writer)") and [Steven Zaillian](/wiki/Steven_Zaillian "Steven Zaillian"), costume designer [Sandy Powell](/wiki/Sandy_Powell_(costume_designer) "Sandy Powell (costume designer)"), production designers [Dante Ferretti](/wiki/Dante_Ferretti "Dante Ferretti") and Bob Shaw, music producer [Robbie Robertson](/wiki/Robbie_Robertson "Robbie Robertson"), and composers [Howard Shore](/wiki/Howard_Shore "Howard Shore")[[203]](#cite_note-203) and [Elmer Bernstein](/wiki/Elmer_Bernstein "Elmer Bernstein").[[204]](#cite_note-204) Schoonmaker, Richardson, Powell, and Ferretti have each won Academy Awards in their respective categories on collaborations with Scorsese. Elaine and [Saul Bass](/wiki/Saul_Bass "Saul Bass"), the latter being Hitchcock's frequent title designer, designed the opening credits for *Goodfellas*, *The Age of Innocence*, *Casino* and *Cape Fear*.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Personal life")]

### Marriages

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Marriages")]

Scorsese has been married five times.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 1965, Scorsese married Laraine Marie Brennan. They have a daughter, Catherine, who was named after Scorsese's mother.[[205]](#cite_note-205) The couple remained together until 1971.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 1976, Scorsese married writer [Julia Cameron](/wiki/Julia_Cameron "Julia Cameron"). They have a daughter, [Domenica Cameron-Scorsese](/wiki/Domenica_Cameron-Scorsese "Domenica Cameron-Scorsese"),[[206]](#cite_note-206) an actress who appeared in *[The Age of Innocence](/wiki/The_Age_of_Innocence_(1993_film) "The Age of Innocence (1993 film)").* After one year of marriage, the couple had an acrimonious divorce which served as the basis of Cameron's first feature, the dark comedy *God's Will*,[[207]](#cite_note-207) which also starred their daughter.[[208]](#cite_note-208)[[209]](#cite_note-success-209) The latter had a small role in *Cape Fear* using the name Domenica Scorsese and has continued to act, write, direct and produce.[[210]](#cite_note-210)

In 1979, Scorsese married actress [Isabella Rossellini](/wiki/Isabella_Rossellini "Isabella Rossellini"). The couple divorced in 1983.[[211]](#cite_note-211)

In March 1983, Scorsese met [Dawn Steel](/wiki/Dawn_Steel "Dawn Steel") (then-junior executive at [Paramount](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures")) at an annual [ShoWest Convention](/wiki/ShoWest_Convention "ShoWest Convention") (in Las Vegas, Nevada), after which the pair began a romantic relationship. Scorsese moved from New York to live in her [Sunset Plaza](/wiki/Sunset_Boulevard "Sunset Boulevard") residence while his [*Last Temptation of Christ*](/wiki/The_Last_Temptation_of_Christ_(film) "The Last Temptation of Christ (film)") was initially in development at Paramount (Steel reportedly recused herself from her boyfriend's passion project). In her 1993 memoir, Steel discussed their relationship, including attending the [Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/Cannes_Film_Festival "Cannes Film Festival") premiere of [*The King of Comedy*](/wiki/The_King_of_Comedy_(film) "The King of Comedy (film)") and later location scouting in [Tunisia](/wiki/Tunisia "Tunisia") together. The two would reconnect professionally in 1987, jump-starting the restoration of [*Lawrence of Arabia*](/wiki/Lawrence_of_Arabia_(film) "Lawrence of Arabia (film)") (shortly after Steel's installation as president at [Columbia Pictures](/wiki/Columbia_Pictures "Columbia Pictures")).[[212]](#cite_note-212)

In 1985, Scorsese married producer [Barbara De Fina](/wiki/Barbara_De_Fina "Barbara De Fina"). The couple divorced in 1991.

From 1989 to 1997, Scorsese was romantically involved with actress [Illeana Douglas](/wiki/Illeana_Douglas "Illeana Douglas").[[213]](#cite_note-213)

In 1999, Scorsese married Helen Schermerhorn Morris. They have a daughter, actress and filmmaker [Francesca](/wiki/Francesca_Scorsese "Francesca Scorsese"), who appeared in his films *[The Departed](/wiki/The_Departed "The Departed")*, *[Hugo](/wiki/Hugo_(film) "Hugo (film)")*, and *[The Aviator](/wiki/The_Aviator_(2004_film) "The Aviator (2004 film)")*, and had a leading role in [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO")/[Sky](/wiki/Sky_Group "Sky Group")'s miniseries *[We Are Who We Are](/wiki/We_Are_Who_We_Are "We Are Who We Are")* in 2020.[[214]](#cite_note-214)[[215]](#cite_note-215)

### Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Politics")]

Scorsese was an opponent of the [Iraq War](/wiki/Iraq_War "Iraq War"), wearing a [white dove](/wiki/Peace_dove "Peace dove") pin to the [75th Academy Awards](/wiki/75th_Academy_Awards "75th Academy Awards") in 2003 and clapping for [Michael Moore](/wiki/Michael_Moore "Michael Moore")'s acceptance speech wherein he criticized President [George W. Bush](/wiki/George_W._Bush "George W. Bush") and the invasion.[[216]](#cite_note-216)[[217]](#cite_note-217) In June 2025, Scorsese said of [Donald Trump's administration](/wiki/Second_presidency_of_Donald_Trump "Second presidency of Donald Trump"): "I do not see compassion in this administration. Indeed, it seems that it takes pleasure in the opposite: hurting, humiliating."[[218]](#cite_note-218)

### Legal issues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Legal issues")]

In March 2024, Scorsese settled a lawsuit with aspiring screenwriter Simon Afram.[[219]](#cite_note-lawsuitoperationfortitude-219)[[220]](#cite_note-aframsettlement-220) Afram accused him of pocketing $500,000 to help handle casting, production and postproduction of his screenplay about World War II's [Operation Fortitude](/wiki/Operation_Fortitude "Operation Fortitude"), which was titled *Operation Fortitude*, only for Scorsese to then do nothing.[[219]](#cite_note-lawsuitoperationfortitude-219)[[220]](#cite_note-aframsettlement-220)

### Religious beliefs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Religious beliefs")]

Scorsese previously identified as a [lapsed Catholic](/wiki/Lapsed_Catholic "Lapsed Catholic"), declaring "I'm a lapsed Catholic. But I am [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic"); there's no way out of it."[[221]](#cite_note-221) In 2016, Scorsese identified himself as a Catholic again, saying, "my way has been, and is, Catholicism. After many years of thinking about other things, dabbling here and there, I am most comfortable as a Catholic. I believe in the tenets of Catholicism."[[32]](#cite_note-Wooden_2016-32)

## Other work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Other work")]

### Film preservation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Film preservation")]

Main articles: [The Film Foundation](/wiki/The_Film_Foundation "The Film Foundation") and [World Cinema Project](/wiki/World_Cinema_Project "World Cinema Project")

Scorsese had been at the forefront in [film preservation](/wiki/Film_preservation "Film preservation") and restoration ever since 1990, when he created [The Film Foundation](/wiki/The_Film_Foundation "The Film Foundation"), a [non-profit](/wiki/Non-profit "Non-profit") film organization which collaborates with film studios to restore prints of old or damaged films. Scorsese launched the organization with [Woody Allen](/wiki/Woody_Allen "Woody Allen"), [Robert Altman](/wiki/Robert_Altman "Robert Altman"), [Francis Ford Coppola](/wiki/Francis_Ford_Coppola "Francis Ford Coppola"), [Clint Eastwood](/wiki/Clint_Eastwood "Clint Eastwood"), [Stanley Kubrick](/wiki/Stanley_Kubrick "Stanley Kubrick"), [George Lucas](/wiki/George_Lucas "George Lucas"), [Sydney Pollack](/wiki/Sydney_Pollack "Sydney Pollack"), [Robert Redford](/wiki/Robert_Redford "Robert Redford"), and [Steven Spielberg](/wiki/Steven_Spielberg "Steven Spielberg"), who all sat on the foundation's original board of directors.[[222]](#cite_note-222) In 2006, [Paul Thomas Anderson](/wiki/Paul_Thomas_Anderson "Paul Thomas Anderson"), [Wes Anderson](/wiki/Wes_Anderson "Wes Anderson"), [Curtis Hanson](/wiki/Curtis_Hanson "Curtis Hanson"), [Peter Jackson](/wiki/Peter_Jackson "Peter Jackson"), [Ang Lee](/wiki/Ang_Lee "Ang Lee") and [Alexander Payne](/wiki/Alexander_Payne "Alexander Payne") joined them.[[223]](#cite_note-Pond-223) In 2015, [Christopher Nolan](/wiki/Christopher_Nolan "Christopher Nolan") also joined the board.[[224]](#cite_note-224) Recent members include [Spike Lee](/wiki/Spike_Lee "Spike Lee"), [Sofia Coppola](/wiki/Sofia_Coppola "Sofia Coppola"), [Guillermo del Toro](/wiki/Guillermo_del_Toro "Guillermo del Toro"), [Barry Jenkins](/wiki/Barry_Jenkins "Barry Jenkins"), [Lynne Ramsay](/wiki/Lynne_Ramsay "Lynne Ramsay"), [Joanna Hogg](/wiki/Joanna_Hogg "Joanna Hogg") and [Kathryn Bigelow](/wiki/Kathryn_Bigelow "Kathryn Bigelow").[[225]](#cite_note-225)

The foundation has restored more than 800 films from around the world and conducts a free educational curriculum for young people on the language and history of film. Scorsese and the Foundation spearheaded fundraising for the film restoration of [Michael Powell](/wiki/Michael_Powell "Michael Powell"), and [Emeric Pressburger](/wiki/Emeric_Pressburger "Emeric Pressburger")'s *[The Red Shoes](/wiki/The_Red_Shoes_(1948_film) "The Red Shoes (1948 film)")* (1948).[[226]](#cite_note-turan-226) For his advocacy in film restoration he received the Robert Osborne Award at the 2018 [TCM Film Festival](/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies "Turner Classic Movies"). The award was given to Scorsese as "an individual who has significantly contributed to preserving the cultural heritage of classic films".[[227]](#cite_note-227)

In November 2020, [The Criterion Channel](/wiki/The_Criterion_Channel "The Criterion Channel") released a 30-minute video titled, *30 Years of The Film Foundation: Martin Scorsese and [Ari Aster](/wiki/Ari_Aster "Ari Aster") in Conversation*, celebrating the "mission, evolution, and ongoing work of The Film Foundation". Scorsese stated as of 2020, the Foundation has helped restore 850 films.

On April 20, 2024, Scorsese partnered with [Seth MacFarlane](/wiki/Seth_MacFarlane "Seth MacFarlane") to showcase *Back From the Ink: Restored Animated Shorts* at the [2024 TCM Classic Film Festival](/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies "Turner Classic Movies"). Macfarlane and Scorsese funded the restoration, and worked with the [UCLA Film and Television Archive](/wiki/UCLA_Film_and_Television_Archive "UCLA Film and Television Archive") and [The Film Foundation](/wiki/The_Film_Foundation "The Film Foundation") with [Paramount Pictures Archives](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures").[[228]](#cite_note-228)[[229]](#cite_note-229)

#### The World Cinema Project

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: The World Cinema Project")]

In 2007, Scorsese established the [World Cinema Project](/wiki/World_Cinema_Project "World Cinema Project") with the mission to preserve and present marginalized and infrequently screened films from regions generally ill-equipped to preserve their own cinema history.[[230]](#cite_note-230) Scorsese's organization has worked with the [Criterion Collection](/wiki/Criterion_Collection "Criterion Collection") to not only preserve the films but to allow them to be released on [DVD](/wiki/DVD "DVD") and [Blu-ray](/wiki/Blu-ray "Blu-ray") boxsets and on streaming services such as [The Criterion Channel](/wiki/The_Criterion_Channel "The Criterion Channel").[[231]](#cite_note-231) Films in the WCP include [Ousmane Sembène](/wiki/Ousmane_Semb%C3%A8ne "Ousmane Sembène")'s *[Black Girl](/wiki/Black_Girl_(1966_film) "Black Girl (1966 film)")* (1966), and [Djibril Diop Mambéty](/wiki/Djibril_Diop_Mamb%C3%A9ty "Djibril Diop Mambéty")'s *[Touki Bouki](/wiki/Touki_Bouki "Touki Bouki")* (1973).

The [Criterion Collection](/wiki/Criterion_Collection "Criterion Collection") so far has released four Vol. boxsets on [DVD](/wiki/DVD "DVD") and [Blu-ray](/wiki/Blu-ray "Blu-ray"), titled, *Martin Scorsese's World Cinema Project*. The first Volume includes 6 titles, *[Touki Bouki](/wiki/Touki_Bouki "Touki Bouki")* (1973), *[Redes](/wiki/Redes_(film) "Redes (film)")* (1936), *[A River Called Titas](/wiki/A_River_Called_Titas "A River Called Titas")* (1973), *[Dry Summer](/wiki/Dry_Summer "Dry Summer")* (1964), *[Trances](/wiki/Trances_(film) "Trances (film)")* (1981) and *[The Housemaid](/wiki/The_Housemaid_(1960_film) "The Housemaid (1960 film)")* (1960).[[232]](#cite_note-232) The second volume also includes 6 titles, *[Insiang](/wiki/Insiang "Insiang")* (1976), *[Mysterious Object at Noon](/wiki/Mysterious_Object_at_Noon "Mysterious Object at Noon")* (2000), *[Revenge](/wiki/Revenge_(1989_film) "Revenge (1989 film)")* (1989), *[Limite](/wiki/Limite "Limite")* (1931), *[Law of the Border](/wiki/Law_of_the_Border "Law of the Border")* (1967), and *[Taipei Story](/wiki/Taipei_Story "Taipei Story")* (1985).[[233]](#cite_note-233) The third volume includes 6 titles as well: *[Lucía](/wiki/Luc%C3%ADa "Lucía")* (1968), *[After the Curfew](/wiki/After_the_Curfew "After the Curfew")* (1954), *[Pixote](/wiki/Pixote "Pixote")* (1980), *[Dos monjes](/wiki/Dos_monjes "Dos monjes")* (1934), *[Soleil Ô](/wiki/Soleil_%C3%94 "Soleil Ô")* (1970), and *[Downpour](/wiki/Downpour_(film) "Downpour (film)")* (1972). The 6 films included in the fourth set are *[Sambizanga](/wiki/Sambizanga "Sambizanga")* (1972), *[Prisioneros de la tierra](/wiki/Prisioneros_de_la_tierra "Prisioneros de la tierra")* (1939), *[Chess of the Wind](/wiki/Chess_of_the_Wind "Chess of the Wind")* (1979), *[Muna Moto](/wiki/Muna_Moto "Muna Moto")* (1975), *Two Girls on the Street* (1939), and *[Kalpana](/wiki/Kalpana_(1948_film) "Kalpana (1948 film)")* (1948).

#### The African Film Heritage Project

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: The African Film Heritage Project")]

In 2017, Scorsese also introduced The African Film Heritage Project (AFHP), which is a joint initiative between Scorsese's non-profit [The Film Foundation](/wiki/The_Film_Foundation "The Film Foundation"), [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO"), [Cineteca di Bologna](/wiki/Cineteca_di_Bologna "Cineteca di Bologna"), and the [Pan African Federation of Filmmakers](/wiki/Pan_African_Federation_of_Filmmakers "Pan African Federation of Filmmakers") (FEPACI).[[234]](#cite_note-234) The project aims to locate and preserve 50 classic African films, some thought lost and others beyond repair, with hopes to make them available to audiences everywhere. In an interview with *Cinema Escapist* in 2018, Scorsese talked about the ambitious collaboration saying, "Our first goal is to launch and conduct a thorough investigation in film archives and laboratories around the world, in order to locate the best surviving elements—original negatives, we hope—for our first 50 titles."[[235]](#cite_note-film-foundation.org-235) He also stated that "Restoration is always the primary goal, of course, but within the initiative, it's also a starting point of a process that follows through with exhibition and dissemination in Africa and abroad. And of course, our restoration process always includes the creation of preservation elements."[[235]](#cite_note-film-foundation.org-235)

In 2019, the AFHP, announced that they would screen restorations of four African films on their home continent for the first time as part of the 50th anniversary of the [Pan African Film Festival](/wiki/Pan_African_Film_Festival "Pan African Film Festival") of [Ouagadougou](/wiki/Ouagadougou "Ouagadougou"). The movies in question are [Med Hondo](/wiki/Med_Hondo "Med Hondo")'s *[Soleil Ô](/wiki/Soleil_%C3%94 "Soleil Ô")* (1970), [Mohammed Lakhdar-Hamina](/wiki/Mohammed_Lakhdar-Hamina "Mohammed Lakhdar-Hamina")'s *[Chronique des années de braise](/wiki/Chronique_des_ann%C3%A9es_de_braise "Chronique des années de braise")* (1975), [Timité Bassori](/wiki/Timit%C3%A9_Bassori "Timité Bassori")'s *La Femme au couteau* (1969), and [Jean-Pierre Dikongue-Pipa](/wiki/Jean-Pierre_Dikongue-Pipa "Jean-Pierre Dikongue-Pipa")'s *[Muna Moto](/wiki/Muna_Moto "Muna Moto")* (1975).[[236]](#cite_note-indiewire.com-236)

### Favorite films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Favorite films")]

Scorsese listed *[Pickup on South Street](/wiki/Pickup_on_South_Street "Pickup on South Street")* as one of his favorite films.[[247]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEWilson2011111-247) *[The Band Wagon](/wiki/The_Band_Wagon "The Band Wagon")* is his favorite musical.[[248]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEWilson201172-248)

In 2012, Scorsese participated in the *[Sight and Sound](/wiki/Sight_and_Sound "Sight and Sound")* [directors' poll](/wiki/The_Sight_%26_Sound_Greatest_Films_of_All_Time_2012#Directors'_poll "The Sight & Sound Greatest Films of All Time 2012"). Held every ten years to select the greatest films of all time, contemporary directors were asked to select ten films of their choice. Scorsese, however, picked 12, which are listed below in alphabetical order:[[249]](#cite_note-BFI2012-249)

* *[2001: A Space Odyssey](/wiki/2001:_A_Space_Odyssey "2001: A Space Odyssey")* (US/UK,1968)
* *[8½](/wiki/8%C2%BD "8½")* (Italy, 1963)
* *[Ashes and Diamonds](/wiki/Ashes_and_Diamonds_(film) "Ashes and Diamonds (film)")* (Poland, 1958)
* *[Citizen Kane](/wiki/Citizen_Kane "Citizen Kane")* (US, 1941)
* *[The Leopard](/wiki/The_Leopard_(1963_film) "The Leopard (1963 film)")* (Italy, 1963)
* *[Paisà](/wiki/Pais%C3%A0 "Paisà")* (Italy, 1946)
* *[The Red Shoes](/wiki/The_Red_Shoes_(1948_film) "The Red Shoes (1948 film)")* (UK, 1948)
* *[The River](/wiki/The_River_(1951_film) "The River (1951 film)")* (US, 1951)
* *[Salvatore Giuliano](/wiki/Salvatore_Giuliano_(film) "Salvatore Giuliano (film)")* (Italy, 1962)
* *[The Searchers](/wiki/The_Searchers "The Searchers")* (US, 1956)
* *[Ugetsu](/wiki/Ugetsu "Ugetsu")* (Japan, 1953)
* *[Vertigo](/wiki/Vertigo_(film) "Vertigo (film)")* (US, 1958)

Ten years later, Scorsese participated again in the [*Sight and Sound* polls](/wiki/The_Sight_and_Sound_Greatest_Films_of_All_Time_2022 "The Sight and Sound Greatest Films of All Time 2022"), picking 15 films, the same 12 of the 2012 list, plus the following:[[250]](#cite_note-250)

* *[Diary of a Country Priest](/wiki/Diary_of_a_Country_Priest "Diary of a Country Priest")* (France, 1951)
* *[Ikiru](/wiki/Ikiru "Ikiru")* (Japan, 1952)
* *[Ordet](/wiki/Ordet "Ordet")* (Denmark, 1955)

In 1999, after the death of [Gene Siskel](/wiki/Gene_Siskel "Gene Siskel"), Scorsese joined [Roger Ebert](/wiki/Roger_Ebert "Roger Ebert") as the guest co-host for an episode of *[Siskel & Ebert](/wiki/Siskel_%26_Ebert "Siskel & Ebert")* where they each stated their 10 favorite films of the 1990s.[[251]](#cite_note-251)[[252]](#cite_note-252) Scorsese's list numerically is:

1. *[The Horse Thief](/wiki/The_Horse_Thief "The Horse Thief")* (China, 1986)
2. *[The Thin Red Line](/wiki/The_Thin_Red_Line_(1998_film) "The Thin Red Line (1998 film)")* (US, 1998)
3. *[A Borrowed Life](/wiki/A_Borrowed_Life "A Borrowed Life")* (Taiwan, 1994)
4. *[Eyes Wide Shut](/wiki/Eyes_Wide_Shut "Eyes Wide Shut")* (US/UK, 1999)
5. *[Bad Lieutenant](/wiki/Bad_Lieutenant "Bad Lieutenant")* (US, 1992)
6. *[Breaking the Waves](/wiki/Breaking_the_Waves "Breaking the Waves")* (Denmark/UK, 1996)
7. *[Bottle Rocket](/wiki/Bottle_Rocket "Bottle Rocket")* (US, 1996)
8. *[Crash](/wiki/Crash_(1996_film) "Crash (1996 film)")* (Canada, 1996)
9. *[Fargo](/wiki/Fargo_(1996_film) "Fargo (1996 film)")* (US, 1996)
10. *[Malcolm X](/wiki/Malcolm_X_(1992_film) "Malcolm X (1992 film)")* (US, 1992) and *[Heat](/wiki/Heat_(1995_film) "Heat (1995 film)")* (US, 1995) (tie)

In 2012, Scorsese recommended 39 foreign films to [Colin Levy](/w/index.php?title=Colin_Levy&action=edit&redlink=1 "Colin Levy (page does not exist)").[[253]](#cite_note-253)

In 2019, Martin Scorsese contributed his list of favorite films to LaCinetek, a streaming platform that compiles film lists from filmmakers worldwide.[[254]](#cite_note-254) As a tireless cinephile, Scorsese submitted two lists: one featuring 73 "founding" films and another "alternative list" with 106 films.[[255]](#cite_note-255) He also included a letter to [Cédric Klapisch](/wiki/C%C3%A9dric_Klapisch "Cédric Klapisch"), one of LaCinetek's founders, explaining his selections and noting that many filmmakers and films he admires are not included in these lists.[[256]](#cite_note-256)

## Legacy and honors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Scorsese&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Legacy and honors")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b2/Martin_Scorsese_02.jpg/250px-Martin_Scorsese_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Martin_Scorsese_02.jpg)

Scorsese receives the [Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement](/wiki/Golden_Lion_for_Lifetime_Achievement "Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement") from actress [Monica Vitti](/wiki/Monica_Vitti "Monica Vitti") at the Venice Film Festival in 1995.

Scorsese's films have been nominated for numerous awards both nationally and internationally, with an Academy Award win for *The Departed*. In 1991, he received the Golden Plate Award of the [American Academy of Achievement](/wiki/American_Academy_of_Achievement "American Academy of Achievement").[[257]](#cite_note-257) In 1997, Scorsese received the [AFI Life Achievement Award](/wiki/AFI_Life_Achievement_Award "AFI Life Achievement Award"). In 1998, the American Film Institute placed three Scorsese films on their list of the greatest American movies: *Raging Bull* at No. 24, *Taxi Driver* at No. 47, and *Goodfellas* at No. 94. For [the tenth-anniversary edition of their list](/wiki/AFI%27s_100_Years...100_Movies_(10th_Anniversary_Edition) "AFI's 100 Years...100 Movies (10th Anniversary Edition)"), *Raging Bull* was moved to No. 4, *Taxi Driver* was moved to No. 52, and *Goodfellas* was moved to No. 92.[[258]](#cite_note-258) In 2001, the American Film Institute placed two Scorsese films on [their list of the most "heart-pounding movies" in American cinema](/wiki/AFI%27s_100_Years_..._100_Thrills "AFI's 100 Years ... 100 Thrills"): *Taxi Driver* at No. 22 and *Raging Bull* at No. 51. At a ceremony in Paris, France, on January 5, 2005, Martin Scorsese was awarded the French [Legion of Honour](/wiki/Legion_of_Honour "Legion of Honour") in recognition of his contribution to cinema. On February 8, 2006, at the [48th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/48th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "48th Annual Grammy Awards"), Scorsese was awarded the [Grammy Award for Best Long Form Music Video](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Long_Form_Music_Video "Grammy Award for Best Long Form Music Video") for *No Direction Home*.

[Lynda Myles](/wiki/Lynda_Myles_(British_producer) "Lynda Myles (British producer)") organized a retrospective of Scorsese's work at the [Edinburgh International Film Festival](/wiki/Edinburgh_International_Film_Festival "Edinburgh International Film Festival") in 1975.[[259]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEWilson201150-259)

In 2007, Scorsese was listed among *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")* magazine's 100 Most Influential People in The World.[[260]](#cite_note-260) In August 2007, Scorsese was named the second-greatest director of all time in a poll by *[Total Film](/wiki/Total_Film "Total Film")* magazine, in front of Steven Spielberg and behind Alfred Hitchcock.[[261]](#cite_note-261) In 2007, Scorsese was honored by the National Italian American Foundation (N.I.A.F.) at the nonprofit's thirty-second Anniversary Gala. During the ceremony, Scorsese helped launch N.I.A.F.'s [Jack Valenti](/wiki/Jack_Valenti "Jack Valenti") Institute in memory of former foundation board member and past president of the Motion Picture Association of America (M.P.A.A.) Jack Valenti. The Institute provides support to Italian film students in the U.S. Scorsese received his award from Mary Margaret Valenti, Jack Valenti's widow. Certain pieces of Scorsese's film-related material and personal papers are contained in the [Wesleyan University](/wiki/Wesleyan_University "Wesleyan University") Cinema Archives, to which scholars and media experts from around the world may have full access.[[262]](#cite_note-262) On September 11, 2007, the [Kennedy Center Honors](/wiki/Kennedy_Center_Honors "Kennedy Center Honors") committee, which recognizes career excellence and cultural influence, named Scorsese as one of the honorees for the year. On June 17, 2008, the American Film Institute placed two of Scorsese's films on the [AFI's 10 Top 10](/wiki/AFI%27s_10_Top_10 "AFI's 10 Top 10") list: *Raging Bull* at number one for the Sports genre and *Goodfellas* at number two for the Gangster genre. In 2013, the staff of *[Entertainment Weekly](/wiki/Entertainment_Weekly "Entertainment Weekly")* voted *Mean Streets* the seventh greatest film ever made.[[263]](#cite_note-263)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fe/President_George_W._Bush_and_Mrs._Laura_Bush_stand_in_the_Blue_Room_of_the_White_House.jpg/250px-President_George_W._Bush_and_Mrs._Laura_Bush_stand_in_the_Blue_Room_of_the_White_House.jpg)](/wiki/File:President_George_W._Bush_and_Mrs._Laura_Bush_stand_in_the_Blue_Room_of_the_White_House.jpg)

[Leon Fleisher](/wiki/Leon_Fleisher "Leon Fleisher"), Martin Scorsese, [Diana Ross](/wiki/Diana_Ross "Diana Ross"), [Brian Wilson](/wiki/Brian_Wilson "Brian Wilson") and [Steve Martin](/wiki/Steve_Martin "Steve Martin") with [President George W. Bush](/wiki/President_George_W._Bush "President George W. Bush") and [Laura Bush](/wiki/Laura_Bush "Laura Bush") in 2007

On January 17, 2010, at the [67th Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/67th_Golden_Globe_Awards "67th Golden Globe Awards"), Scorsese was the recipient of the [Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Cecil_B._DeMille_Award "Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award"). On September 18, 2011, at the [63rd Primetime Emmy Awards](/wiki/63rd_Primetime_Emmy_Awards "63rd Primetime Emmy Awards"), Scorsese won the [Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Directing for a Drama Series](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Directing_for_a_Drama_Series "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Directing for a Drama Series") for his work on [the series premiere](/wiki/Boardwalk_Empire_(episode) "Boardwalk Empire (episode)") of *Boardwalk Empire*. In 2011, Scorsese received an honorary doctorate from the [National Film School in Lodz](/wiki/National_Film_School_in_Lodz "National Film School in Lodz"). At the awards ceremony he said, "I feel like I'm a part of this school and that I attended it," paying tribute to the films of Wajda, Munk, Has, Polanski and Skolimowski.[[264]](#cite_note-264) [King Missile](/wiki/King_Missile "King Missile") wrote "[Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese_(song) "Martin Scorsese (song)")" in his honor. On February 12, 2012, at the [65th British Academy Film Awards](/wiki/65th_British_Academy_Film_Awards "65th British Academy Film Awards"), Scorsese was the recipient of the [BAFTA Academy Fellowship Award](/wiki/BAFTA_Academy_Fellowship_Award "BAFTA Academy Fellowship Award").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/44/MartinScorseseHWoFOct10.jpg/250px-MartinScorseseHWoFOct10.jpg)](/wiki/File:MartinScorseseHWoFOct10.jpg)

Scorsese's motion picture star on the [Hollywood Walk of Fame](/wiki/Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame "Hollywood Walk of Fame")

On September 16, 2012, Scorsese won two [Emmy Awards](/wiki/64th_Primetime_Creative_Arts_Emmy_Awards#Directing "64th Primetime Creative Arts Emmy Awards") for Outstanding Directing for Nonfiction Programming and Outstanding Nonfiction Special for his work on the documentary *George Harrison: Living in the Material World*.[[265]](#cite_note-265) In 2013, the [National Endowment for the Humanities](/wiki/National_Endowment_for_the_Humanities "National Endowment for the Humanities") selected Scorsese for the [Jefferson Lecture](/wiki/Jefferson_Lecture "Jefferson Lecture"), the U.S. federal government's highest honor for achievement in the [humanities](/wiki/Humanities "Humanities"). He was the first filmmaker chosen for the honor.[[266]](#cite_note-266) His lecture, delivered on April 1, 2013, at the [John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts](/wiki/John_F._Kennedy_Center_for_the_Performing_Arts "John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts"), was titled "Persistence of Vision: Reading the Language of Cinema".[[267]](#cite_note-267) Scorsese was appointed to the Polish [Gold Medal for Merit to Culture – Gloria Artis](/wiki/Medal_for_Merit_to_Culture_%E2%80%93_Gloria_Artis "Medal for Merit to Culture – Gloria Artis") on April 11, 2017, in recognition of his contribution to Polish cinema.[[268]](#cite_note-268) He received the Medal on May 29, 2025.[[269]](#cite_note-269)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/48/Jon_Stewart_and_Martin_Scorsese_%288250488258%29.jpg/250px-Jon_Stewart_and_Martin_Scorsese_%288250488258%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Jon_Stewart_and_Martin_Scorsese_(8250488258).jpg)

[Jon Stewart](/wiki/Jon_Stewart "Jon Stewart") with Scorsese at the [Peabody Awards](/wiki/Peabody_Awards "Peabody Awards") in 2006

Scorsese has also garnered favorable responses from numerous film giants including Ingmar Bergman,[[270]](#cite_note-270) [Frank Capra](/wiki/Frank_Capra "Frank Capra"),[[271]](#cite_note-271) Jean-Luc Godard,[[272]](#cite_note-272) [Werner Herzog](/wiki/Werner_Herzog "Werner Herzog"),[[273]](#cite_note-273) [Elia Kazan](/wiki/Elia_Kazan "Elia Kazan"),[[274]](#cite_note-274) Akira Kurosawa,[[275]](#cite_note-275) [David Lean](/wiki/David_Lean "David Lean"),[[276]](#cite_note-276) Michael Powell,[[277]](#cite_note-277) Satyajit Ray,[[278]](#cite_note-278) and [François Truffaut](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Truffaut "François Truffaut").[[279]](#cite_note-279) He was elected to the [American Philosophical Society](/wiki/American_Philosophical_Society "American Philosophical Society") in 2008.[[280]](#cite_note-280) He was awarded an [Honorary Doctorate](/wiki/Honorary_Doctorate "Honorary Doctorate") from the [University of Oxford](/wiki/University_of_Oxford "University of Oxford") on June 20, 2018.[[281]](#cite_note-281) As of 2021, four of Scorsese's films (*Mean Streets*, *Taxi Driver*, *Raging Bull*, and *Goodfellas*) have been selected by the [Library of Congress](/wiki/Library_of_Congress "Library of Congress") for preservation in the [National Film Registry](/wiki/National_Film_Registry "National Film Registry") for being "culturally, historically, or aesthetically significant".[[282]](#cite_note-282) In commenting on Scorsese's 2019 film *The Irishman*, [Guillermo del Toro](/wiki/Guillermo_del_Toro "Guillermo del Toro") cited Scorsese's ability as a director for the depiction of [character development](/wiki/Character_arc "Character arc") comparable to the films of "Renoir, Bresson, Bergman, Oliveira or Kurosawa".[[283]](#cite_note-283) [Sam Mendes](/wiki/Sam_Mendes "Sam Mendes"), in his acceptance speech after winning the 2020 [Golden Globe Award for Best Director](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_Best_Director "Golden Globe Award for Best Director") for *[1917](/wiki/1917_(2019_film) "1917 (2019 film)")*, praised Scorsese's contribution to cinema, stating, "There's not one director in this room, not one director in the world, that is not in the shadow of Martin Scorsese... I just have to say that."[[284]](#cite_note-284) [Bong Joon-ho](/wiki/Bong_Joon-ho "Bong Joon-ho"), in his acceptance speech for the 2020 [Academy Award for Best Director](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Director "Academy Award for Best Director") for *[Parasite](/wiki/Parasite_(2019_film) "Parasite (2019 film)")*, said, "When I was young and studying cinema, there was a saying that I carved deep into my heart, which is, the most personal is the most creative." He then said that this quote had come from Scorsese, which prompted the audience to give Scorsese a standing ovation.[[285]](#cite_note-285)

In 2021, lifelong friend [George Lucas](/wiki/George_Lucas "George Lucas") and his wife [Mellody Hobson](/wiki/Mellody_Hobson "Mellody Hobson") through the Hobson/Lucas Family Foundation made a donation to NYU to establish the Martin Scorsese Institute of Global Cinematic Arts.[[286]](#cite_note-286)

On October 17, 2025, Apple TV premiered a five-part documentary series about Scorsese titled *[Mr. Scorsese](/wiki/Mr._Scorsese "Mr. Scorsese")*, directed by [Rebecca Miller](/wiki/Rebecca_Miller "Rebecca Miller").[[287]](#cite_note-287)
